message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,400+ reviews; OpenTable ~2,979 reviews (4.7); Tripadvisor 251 reviews (4.1, #16 of 127 in La Quinta)
- Sentiment: Mostly positive — strong on OpenTable (4.7) and Yelp (~4.4); more mixed on Tripadvisor (4.1) with service-inconsistency complaints alongside food praise
- Longevity: Opened in La Quinta in 2005; ~21 years operating under same ownership (Sam and Jay Lee); Laguna Beach sister location opened 2013 but Yelp now shows it as CLOSED
- Credentials: ["Desert Sun 'Best of the Valley' — Japanese restaurant category (multi-year winner, reported as 7-8+ consecutive years) [UNVERIFIED-exact-count]","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice badge","Self-reported 'Best Sushi & Japanese in the Desert' (Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' / 2021 Best of Desert Awards claimed on own site) [UNVERIFIED-independent]"]
- Appears in queries: MIXED. Surfaces on broad 'best sushi La Quinta' and 'sushi near Palm Desert' aggregator-driven queries (OpenTable, Tripadvisor, resorthd.com). NOT mentioned in editorial 'best sushi Coachella Valley 2025' lists — Palm Springs Life's Asian roundup, Visit Palm Springs' best sushi list, and PS Tribune's 5 must-try Japanese spots all omit Okura, favoring Sandfish, Ponzu, Otori, Taka Shin, Musashi, Shabu Shabu Zen, Kobe Steakhouse. Loses the editorial conversation despite winning the local-paper popularity vote.
- Site structured data: okurasushi.com presents clean human-readable hours, address, phone, and menu sections, but no detectable JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup surfaced via fetch. Site is e-commerce-enabled (online ordering, gift cards, reservations) but the structured signals an agent would parse cleanly (Restaurant schema, Menu schema, opening hours specification) appear absent.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.query: best sushi Coachella Valley 2025 La Quinta Japanese restaurant

content.content:
# Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar - Key Information

## Location & Contact
- **Address:** 78480 HWY 111, La Quinta, CA 92253
- **Phone:** (760) 564-5820
- **Email:** info@okurasushi.com

## Hours of Operation
- **Mon-Thu:** 3:30 PM – 9:00 PM
- **Fri-Sat:** 3:30 PM – 9:30 PM
- **Sun:** 3:30 PM – 9:00 PM
- **Happy Hour:** Sun-Thu, 3:30 PM – 5:00 PM

## Restaurant Highlights
The establishment describes itself as offering "a fusion of Japanese and California cuisine" in a contemporary space with L.A./Hollywood aesthetic. Notable features include signature décor elements and daily Grade A fish deliveries from Japan.

## Awards & Recognition
- Voted Best Sushi & Japanese in the Desert
- Featured in Palm Springs Life Best of the Best
- 2021 Best of Desert Awards recipient

## Services Offered
- Dine-in with reservations (OpenTable)
- Online ordering (ToastTab)
- Digital gift cards
- Catering services
- Full bar with signature cocktails and premium sakes

## Menu Categories
Offerings include sushi, robata grill skewers, salads, Kobe steak, tempura, and various fish/meat entrées.

**Note:** No JSON-LD structured data or schema.org markup was detected in the provided HTML source.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best sushi and Japanese restaurants in the Coachella Valley and La Quinta area:

## Top Recommendations

**Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar (La Quinta)**
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar in La Quinta offers a truly exceptional Japanese specialty experience with a chic atmosphere adorned with a life-size petrified Japanese cherry blossom tree and a captivating "Geisha Angel" mural. Guests rave about the "fresh and delicious" sushi, with standout dishes like the Hamachi Jalapeno rolls and sashimi platters praised for their "high quality" and "stunning presentation."

**The Venue Sushi & Spirits Redefined (Palm Desert)**
The Venue Sushi & Spirits Redefined in Palm Desert offers an exquisite journey into authentic Japanese cuisine, standing as a "gem in the desert," and has earned the title of "the BEST sushi restaurant in the valley."

**Sawa Sushi (La Quinta)**
Sawa Sushi, located at 78995 CA-111 STE 6, La Quinta, CA, is a hidden gem that delivers an exceptional sushi experience with a focus on freshness and quality, featuring an intimate, friendly atmosphere.

**Misaki Sushi & Grill (La Quinta)**
Misaki Sushi & Grill offers the city of La Quinta artistic and large portion sushi rolls as well as fresh sushi, sashimi and kitchen entrees all at an affordable price in a relaxed comfortable setting.

**Coral Sushi & Sake Bar (La Quinta)**
Coral Sushi & Sake Bar, nestled between La Quinta and Indio, offers an exciting fusion of sushi with a distinct Korean influence, creating a dining experience that's both authentic and innovative.

Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar is located at 78480 Hwy 111, La Quinta, CA 92253 and has 1567 Photos and 1439 Reviews on Yelp.

## Restaurant Highlights

The restaurant serves a fusion of Japanese and California cuisine in a chic contemporary restaurant space that has an L.A./Hollywood vibe. Okura is the only sushi restaurant in the region to receive daily deliveries of Grade A fish and seafood coming in from Japan.

## Customer Reviews

Based on the search results, Okura receives very positive reviews across multiple platforms. On OpenTable, Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar is rated 4.7 stars by 2979 diners. On Sirved, it has earned an average rating of 4.4 stars.

Reviewers consistently praise its "freshest sushi," "beautiful presentation," and "exceptional service." Beyond an expansive sushi menu, there is something for everyone including skewers from the robata grill, salads, Kobe steak, tempura and chicken, beef and fish entrees.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar in La Quinta on Tripadvisor:

**Ranking and Reviews:**
Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar is ranked #18 on Tripadvisor among 108 restaurants in La Quinta with 251 unbiased reviews.

**Restaurant Description:**
Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar serves up a fusion of Japanese and California cuisine in a chic contemporary restaurant space that has an L.A./Hollywood vibe. Custom décor includes gold Kriskadecor chains hanging from the ceiling, a 4-foot high bronze Buddha and a life-size, petrified Japanese cherry blossom tree at the center of the restaurant.

**Menu Offerings:**
Beyond an expansive sushi menu, there is something for everyone including skewers from the Robata Grill, Salads, Toban Yaki Steak, Tempura, Chicken, Beef and Fish Entrees, and of course, delicious desserts like Green tea Panna Cotta.

The reviews showcase mixed experiences, with many praising the food quality and ambiance, while some mention service inconsistencies.

Links: [{"title":"OKURA ROBATA GRILL & SUSHI - CLOSED - Updated March 2026 - 465 Photos & 488 Reviews - 858 S Coast Hwy, Laguna Beach, California - Japanese - Restaurant Reviews - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/okura-robata-grill-and-sushi-laguna-beach"},{"title":"Okura Sushi - 355 Reviews - Sushi Bars in Laguna Beach, CA - Birdeye","url":"https://reviews.birdeye.com/okura-sushi-146837948668494"},{"title":"Okura Robata Grill & Sushi - Laguna Beach, CA Restaurant","url":"https://www.seamless.com/menu/okura-robata-grill--sushi-858-s-coast-hwy-laguna-beach/546097"},{"title":"Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar, Laguna Beach, CA - Reviews, Ratings, Tips and Why You Should Go – Wanderlog","url":"https://wanderlog.com/place/details/1294918/okura-robata-grill--sushi-bar"},{"title":"It's official: San Shi Go 060821 - Front Page","url":"https://www.stunewslagunaarchives.com/index.php/archives-prior-to-2024/front-page-archive/17832-it-s-official-san-shi-go-060821"},{"title":"Breaking News: OTO Sushi Opens Tonight (Monday)","url":"https://bestoflagunabeach.substack.com/p/breaking-news-oto-sushi-opens-tonight"},{"title":"Instagram","url":"https://www.instagram.com/explore/locations/237390561/okura-sushi-laguna-beach/?hl=en"},{"title":"Home - Best Sushi Desert - Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar","url":"https://okurasushi.com/"},{"title":"San Shi Go Closing Sept. 17th, 2023 - The Best of Laguna Beach","url":"https://thebestoflagunabeach.com/san-shi-go-closing-sept-17th-2023/"},{"title":"Menu - Hot Cold Edamame - Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar","url":"https://okurasushi.com/menu/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Okura in Laguna Beach:

Okura Robata Grill & Sushi is permanently closed. The Okura restaurant closed in November 2019 after a multi-year stint, though another source indicates it closed in February 2021.

Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar was a high-end sushi restaurant located on Coast Highway that offered a variety of options including rolls, carpaccio, sashimi and more. Okura was a tenant for four years, and the landlord noted they felt it was better to move on.

The location at 858 S Coast Highway was later occupied by San Shi Go sushi restaurant, which itself permanently closed on Saturday, Sept. 16th, 2023.

Links: [{"title":"Jay Lee - President - Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar | LinkedIn","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/in/jay-lee-805ab47b/"},{"title":"About – Pokehana","url":"https://pokehana.com/about/"},{"title":"The Allure of Okura | Eat & Drink | lagunabeachindy.com","url":"https://www.lagunabeachindy.com/laguna_beach_magazine/eat_drink/the-allure-of-okura/article_8d194761-732d-5e24-8b75-0d6500926abf.html"},{"title":"Menus for Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar - La Quinta - La Quinta - SinglePlatform","url":"http://places.singleplatform.com/okura-robata-grill/menu"},{"title":"Okura Robata Grill and Sushi Bar - La Quinta Restaurant - La Quinta, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/okura-robata-grill-and-sushi-bar-la-quinta"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Okura in La Quinta and its owners:

Okura Robata Grill is a Japanese-fusion restaurant owned by husband and wife, Sam and Jay Lee. Jay Lee is the owner of Okura Sushi and believes that true chefs are always learning and working to improve their techniques and concepts.

However, the search results do not contain specific information about when Okura in La Quinta opened in 2005. The results mention that a location opened quietly in early October with owner Jay Lee ensuring the service and food were on par with the quality of the La Quinta location (referring to a Laguna Beach expansion), but the original 2005 opening date for the La Quinta restaurant is not explicitly confirmed in these search results.

Based on the search results, I found information from Palm Springs Life about the best Japanese and sushi restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2025:

## Palm Springs Life's 2025 Best Asian Cuisine Winners

Palm Springs Life invites readers to vote for their favorite businesses in their annual Best of the Best competition, and this year's winners for Best Asian Cuisine are featured, with 2025 winners across all categories available.

## Featured Japanese & Sushi Restaurants

According to Palm Springs Life's roundup, several notable establishments are highlighted:

**Nobu** - Chef Nobu Matsuhisa serves popular Japanese dishes like yellowtail jalapeño and rock shrimp tempura inside Stadium 2 at Indian Wells Tennis Garden year-round, and Nobu wins Best Asian Cuisine in Indian Wells.

**Sushi Bars** - Fresh sashimi, creative rolls, and impressive sushi boats define popular sushi bars in the area, with guests loving the fast service and quality sushi prepared by skilled chefs.

**Fantasy Springs Resort Casino** - The menu at this Fantasy Springs Resort Casino restaurant spans several cuisines, from Japanese sushi to Chinese dim sum.

The article provides a comprehensive guide to Asian dining options across multiple cities in the Coachella Valley, with sushi being prominently featured among the best restaurants in the region.

input.prompt: List the 5 Japanese spots featured. Is Okura mentioned?
List all sushi restaurants mentioned. Is Okura La Quinta mentioned?

content.content:
# The 5 Japanese Spots Featured

1. **Shabu Shabu Zen** (Rancho Mirage) - "interactive experience of Japanese hot pot"
2. **Ponzu Sushi** (Palm Springs) - "expertly crafted sushi in a sleek and modern setting"
3. **Kobe Japanese Steakhouse** (Rancho Mirage) - teppanyaki restaurant with chef performances
4. **Otori Sushi** (Cathedral City) - "hidden gem beloved by locals"
5. **Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey** (Palm Springs) - upscale sushi paired with whiskey

## Is Okura Mentioned?

No, Okura is not mentioned anywhere in this article.

# Sushi Restaurants Mentioned

The article lists five sushi restaurants in Palm Springs:

1. **Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey** - "elevated omakase and Japanese whisky lovers"
2. **Taka Shin Japanese Cuisine** - described as a "downtown favorite with loyal locals"
3. **Joyce's Sushi** - known for "inventive specialty rolls and fresh ingredients"
4. **Ponzu Sushi** - characterized as "comfortable, consistent, and crowd-pleasing"
5. **Happy Sushi Palm Springs** - described as "fun, fast, and full of flavor"

## Okura La Quinta

**No**, Okura La Quinta is not mentioned in this article. The guide focuses exclusively on the five sushi establishments listed above.

content.text: I have enough independent verification to assess the claims.

confirms: Yelp ~1,439 reviews (claimed 1,400+ — confirmed; current Yelp listing shows 1,439 reviews + 1,567 photos)
OpenTable rating 4.7 with ~2,979 reviews — confirmed via multiple search results
Laguna Beach sister location is CLOSED — Yelp confirms CLOSED status (closed in 2019-2021 per archive sources)
Owners Sam and Jay Lee — confirmed via LinkedIn and Pokehana profile (Jay Lee listed as President of Okura Robata Grill & Sushi Bar)
Desert Sun 'Best of the Valley' multi-year winner — confirmed; one source explicitly states 'over 8 years' first place in Japanese category
Editorial omission from PS Tribune '5 Must-Try Japanese Spots' — confirmed: list is Shabu Shabu Zen, Ponzu, Kobe, Otori, Sandfish; Okura NOT mentioned
Editorial omission from Visit Palm Springs best sushi list — confirmed: list is Sandfish, Taka Shin, Joyce's, Ponzu, Happy Sushi; Okura NOT mentioned
Surfaces strongly on aggregator queries (Yelp, OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Wanderlog) for 'best sushi La Quinta' / 'Coachella Valley'
No JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup detected on okurasushi.com — confirmed via WebFetch
Site is e-commerce-enabled with ToastTab online ordering, OpenTable reservations, gift cards — confirmed on homepage
Mixed Tripadvisor sentiment with service-inconsistency complaints alongside food praise — confirmed in review snippets ('good sushi, horrible service' is a literal review title)
contradicts: Tripadvisor rank: claim says '#16 of 127 in La Quinta' but current Tripadvisor search result shows '#18 of 108 restaurants in La Quinta' with 251 reviews. Review count (251) and 4.1 rating align, but rank and denominator differ — actual is #18 of 108, not #16 of 127.
flagged_as_unverified: Exact 2005 La Quinta opening date — could not independently confirm; no source in search results states the founding year explicitly (only confirms Lee ownership)
Yelp average rating of ~4.4 — review count confirmed, but exact star rating not surfaced in Yelp listing snippet (Sirved cites 4.4 but that's a different aggregator)

Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' / 2021 Best of Desert Awards — claimed on own site and restauranteur.com profile, but could not independently fetch the Palm Springs Life roundup (403 Forbidden) to confirm Okura is currently listed as a winner; the PSL Asian roundup article exists but content not retrievable
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice badge — not explicitly seen in search results (Tripadvisor listing confirmed but badge status not surfaced)

Exact count of Desert Sun consecutive wins (7-8+) — one source says 'over 8 years' but this appears to be from a real-estate blog (yourresorthome.com), not Desert Sun directly; exact year-by-year tally not independently verifiable from a primary source
input.confidence:
high</confidence>
<parameter name="notes">Core visibility thesis holds strongly: Okura wins the aggregator/popularity layer (massive Yelp/OpenTable footprint, multi-year Desert Sun winner) but loses the editorial layer (omitted from PS Tribune and Visit Palm Springs roundups — both independently confirmed). The 'no JSON-LD' finding is verified — site is e-commerce-functional but agent-unparseable for structured data. The Laguna Beach closure is verified. Only material correction: Tripadvisor rank is #18 of 108 (not #16 of 127) — likely a stale snapshot in the brief. Visibility-gap-score of 7 and 'convertible' tier are well-supported by the structured-data absence combined with editorial absence despite high review volume.
